detected.llm_rationale: Clean all-cash PE take-private (Altaris, healthcare-sim software; definitive agmt Jun 16). Break risk low — no antitrust angle, Altaris well-capitalised. But spread is only +0.65%, BELOW the 1% MIN_SPREAD floor: the market has already compressed it. No arb edge left to harvest — drop, don't deploy real capital + slippage for a near-certain 0.65%.

All-cash PE buyout (American Industrial Partners, $1.272B, 72% premium; definitive Apr 14, close H2-26 pending routine regulatory). Med-device, no plausible antitrust block; near-par price signals the market prices it near-certain. Spread +0.28% — essentially closed, far below the 1% floor. Drop: no residual spread.

All-cash PE take-private (Searchlight+Abry, $726M; definitive Feb 26, NO financing condition — a plus). Only residual risk is the majority-of-minority vote, but the ~700% premium all but guarantees approval. Old deal near close; spread +0.22%. Drop: spent spread, no edge.
